title: XXXIV
html:
XXXIV

вАФпїњвАФпїњBut it is an indubitable verity, continued I, addressing myself to the commissary, changing only the form of my asseverationпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњthat I owe the king of France nothing but my goodwill; for he is a very honest man, and I wish him all health and pastime in the worldпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

Pardonnez moiпїњвАФreplied the commissary, you are indebted to him six livres four sous, for the next post from hence to St.¬†Fons, in your route to AvignonпїњвАФwhich being a post royal, you pay double for the horses and postillionпїњвАФotherwise вАЩtwould have amounted to no more than three livres two sousпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

вАФпїњвАФпїњBut I donвАЩt go by land; said I.

вАФпїњвАФпїњYou may if you please; replied the commissaryпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

Your most obedient servantпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњsaid I, making him a low bowпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

The commissary, with all the sincerity of grave good breedingпїњвАФmade me one, as low again.пїњвАФпїњвАФпїњI never was more disconcerted with a bow in my life.

вАФпїњвАФпїњThe devil take the serious character of these people! quoth IпїњвАФ(aside) they understand no more of irony than thisпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

The comparison was standing close by with his panniersпїњвАФbut something sealвАЩd up my lipsпїњвАФI could not pronounce the nameпїњвАФ

Sir, said I, collecting myselfпїњвАФit is not my intention to take postпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

вАФBut you mayпїњвАФsaid he, persisting in his first replyпїњвАФyou may take post if you chooseпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

вАФAnd I may take salt to my pickled herring, said I, if I chooseпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

вАФBut I do not chooseпїњвАФ

вАФBut you must pay for it, whether you do or no.

Aye! for the salt; said I (I know)пїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

вАФAnd for the post too; added he. Defend me! cried IпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

I travel by waterпїњвАФI am going down the Rh√іne this very afternoonпїњвАФmy baggage is in the boatпїњвАФand I have actually paid nine livres for my passageпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

CвАЩest tout egalпїњвАФвАЩtis all one; said he.

Bon Dieu! what, pay for the way I go! and for the way I do not¬†go!

вАФпїњвАФпїњCвАЩest tout egal; replied the commissaryпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

вАФпїњвАФпїњThe devil it is! said IпїњвАФbut I will go to ten thousand Bastiles firstпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

O England! England! thou land of liberty, and climate of good sense, thou tenderest of mothersпїњвАФand gentlest of nurses, cried I, kneeling upon one knee, as I was beginning my apostrophe.

When the director of Madam Le BlancвАЩs conscience coming in at that instant, and seeing a person in black, with a face as pale as ashes, at his devotionsпїњвАФlooking still paler by the contrast and distress of his draperyпїњвАФaskвАЩd, if I stood in want of the aids of the churchпїњвАФпїњвАФпїњ

I go by waterпїњвАФsaid IпїњвАФand hereвАЩs another will be for making me pay for going by oil.
